headline: Oil Giant Emerges As An AI Power Play; Two Key Suppliers Rally
summary: Chevron announced Monday that it will power a huge Microsoft data center in West Texas with natural gas, using gas turbines supplied by GE Vernova and Caterpillar.  GE Vernova stock rose near a buy point, and Caterpillar stock jumped.  Project Kilby, as it is known, is expected to produce 2.7 gigawatts of electricity.

headline: Microsoft Signs 20-Year Power Deal With Chevron Showing How Far AI’s Energy Needs Have Grown
summary: CNBC’s Brian Sullivan walked viewers through a landmark energy agreement that paints the picture for how much electricity the AI buildout actually needs. Chevron has signed a 20-year power purchase agreement with Microsoft to supply natural-gas-fired electricity to a Microsoft data center in far west Texas, about an hour southwest of Odessa. headline: GE Vernova (GEV) Launches Microsoft Data Center Power Venture With Joulent
summary: GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) and Joulent launch an AI focused energy collaboration to supply multi gigawatt power for Microsoft data centers. Joulent, backed by GE Vernova and Engine No. 1, targets reliable capacity for hyperscale data centers supporting Microsoft’s AI workloads with decarbonisation objectives. GE Vernova reports additional progress across grid modernization contracts, nuclear manufacturing partnerships in Europe and Ontario, and sustainability initiatives. For investors tracking...

headline: Joulent Launches to Deliver Multi-Gigawatt Power Solutions for American Innovation
summary: HOUSTON, June 22, 2026--Joulent, LLC ("the Company" or "Joulent") today announced its launch as a technology-driven energy company purpose-built to deliver reliable, multi-gigawatt power at the speed and scale demanded by the current pace of innovation. Founded and developed over three years by Engine No. 1 in strategic collaboration with GE Vernova, Joulent will provide reliable, continuous power engineered to meet the needs of American innovation.
